quotes.quote: Specifically, it uses the classic Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) algorithm to select the most promising record.
quotes.rationale: Identifies the implemented selection algorithm and its ranking role.

quotes.quote: Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.
quotes.rationale: Shows that the selection mechanism controls which generated ideas advance to experimental validation.

quotes.quote: The architecture of DeepScientist actualizes the Bayesian Optimization loop through a multi-agent system equipped with an open-knowledge system and a continuously accumulating Findings Memory. This memory is composed of both frontier human knowledge (e.g., papers and codes) and the system's own historical findings, and it intelligently guides subsequent explorations.
quotes.rationale: Describes the memory structure and its role in guiding later exploration.

quotes.quote: This deeply validated record becomes a new record in the system's knowledge base, thus influencing the decision-making process in all subsequent cycles.
quotes.rationale: Shows admission of validated records into memory and subsequent policy influence.

quotes.quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
quotes.rationale: Reports the command-line re-execution validator, its executability target, and the false-positive failure it addresses.

quotes.quote: Three human experts supervise the process to verify outputs and filter out hallucinations.
quotes.rationale: Identifies human expert validation of outputs and hallucination filtering.

quotes.quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
quotes.rationale: Adds manual inspection of experimental results for authenticity.

quotes.quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.
quotes.rationale: Describes an AI reviewer benchmark comparing outputs against public prior work.

quotes.quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.
quotes.rationale: Reports a human program committee used for more rigorous terminal assessment.

quotes.quote: To make the AI a more capable partner, future work should focus on key enhancements: developing simulated discovery environments to accelerate learning via reinforcement, creating frameworks for integrating feedback from the scientific community, and ultimately, bridging the gap to the physical sciences through robotics.
quotes.rationale: Identifies proposed, future feedback and fidelity extensions rather than implemented validation channels.

diagnostics.consequence: Executability claims from initial implementation completion cannot be accepted without independent re-execution because a large share of attempts reportedly failed to complete.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports a high false-positive rate for initial implementation completion.

diagnostics.consequence: The major internal selection and memory-update channels cannot be coded as validated or calibrated for their target properties, limiting confidence in automated advancement and reuse decisions.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Specifically, it uses the classic Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) algorithm to select the most promising record.
This deeply validated record becomes a new record in the system's knowledge base, thus influencing the decision-making process in all subsequent cycles.

Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports selection and memory influence but does not report reliability evidence for the selection mechanism or memory admission decisions.

diagnostics.consequence: Terminal quality comparisons may support aggregate assessment, but they should not be treated as closed-loop control over search or as reliability evidence for internal novelty filtering.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports terminal DeepReviewer and program-committee assessments, but not that those assessments feed back into search control.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.

recommendations.proposed_action: Keep independent command-line re-execution as a required gate after implementation completion and report pass/fail counts separately from initial completion reports.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Re-execution results are reported for every implementation attempt and distinguish initial completion from verified completion.
success_check.evidence_needed: Execution logs and aggregate pass/fail counts for initial completion and secondary re-execution.
success_check.observable: Verified completion rate after secondary command-line re-execution

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Specifically, it uses the classic Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) algorithm to select the most promising record.

Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.

recommendations.proposed_action: Evaluate the selection mechanism with a property-specific study that reports how UCB-selected records compare with rejected or alternative-ranked records on downstream scientific progress.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Selected records outperform a stated rejected or alternative-ranked comparator on downstream progress.
success_check.evidence_needed: Selection logs, comparator cohorts, and downstream progress outcomes for selected and non-selected records.
success_check.observable: Downstream scientific-progress yield by selection status or rank

exact_quotes.exact_quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report terminal DeepReviewer and program-committee assessments as terminal external assessments unless a documented routing path shows their outputs changing search, memory, or stage decisions.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Each assessment is labeled with a documented routing path; terminal-only assessments are not counted as search control.
success_check.evidence_needed: Pipeline documentation or logs showing whether assessment outputs feed search, memory admission, stage transition, or terminal reporting only.
success_check.observable: Documented feedback path for each assessment channel

memory_structure: frontier human knowledge including papers and codes
deeply validated records admitted to the system knowledge base
